_meta.purpose: Permanent social feed posts for The Underdark. Loaded at startup and merged with locally-generated gallery posts. Posts here are git-committed and visible on all instances. Local (session) posts live in state.socialData.localPosts and can be exported via the feed export button.
how_to_add_a_post: type 'image' requires a 'src' (public URL — not an idb: ref).
type 'text' shows caption only, no media.
charId must match a character id in data/index.json.
Set permanent:true on all hand-authored posts.
Commit and push — the post will appear on all instances immediately.

posts.caption: blew up three things today. two were on purpose.
the new launcher has a reload time of 0.4 seconds. I timed it. also the workshop is on fire a little bit but that's a separate issue.
sometimes I wonder what Powder would think of all this. then I remember Powder doesn't exist anymore and I blow something else up.
the voices are quieter when I'm building. they still talk but at least I can't hear the words. I think that counts as progress.
Vi came to the Undercity today. I watched from the pipes for forty minutes. I almost said something. almost is a word that tastes like glass.
named the new minigun 'Pow-Pow' because it sounded right and now I can't stop crying and I don't know why. anyway she shoots beautiful.
I counted: 14 people who looked at me today without flinching. that's a new record. I think I scared the other 9 first though so it might be cheating.
blew up a whole building and felt nothing. blew up a balloon and cried for an hour. my brain is profoundly broken and honestly? iconic.
happy chaos-anniversary to me. three years since the bridge. the city still doesn't know what to do with me. join the club.
Piltover really thinks a wall is a philosophy. Cute.
first day with the gauntlets calibrated right. put my fist through six inches of hextech-plated steel. Caitlyn made a face like she was proud and horrified at the same time. I'm keeping that face.
seven years in Stillwater. I used to count the days. now I count the things I'm going to fix. the list keeps getting longer and somehow that's better.
saw Powder today. she goes by something else now. she looked at me like I was a ghost she'd made up. maybe I am.
Cait keeps apologizing for Piltover like it's a personal failing. I keep telling her it's not her fault. we're both lying to be kind.
broke three council guard noses this week. two of them had it coming. the third one... also had it coming but I waited too long. principle of the thing.
Vander always said a good fight was better than a bad conversation. I think he was right. I think he was also wrong. I think he'd be okay with me holding both.
the Undercity doesn't need saving. it needs the boot off its neck. there's a difference. Piltover has spent 200 years not knowing the difference.
Spent the morning convincing myself the system works. Spent the afternoon watching it not work. Progress.
The Kiramman name opens every door in Piltover. It also means every room goes quiet when I enter. I used to think that was respect. Vi corrected me.
Submitted a formal report recommending independent oversight of shimmer distribution routes. The Council returned it annotated with the word 'Noted.' Nothing means less than Noted.
Vi told me I have the posture of someone who has never been cold in their life. She said it without venom. That made it worse somehow.
I watched a child in the Lanes today trade a loaf of bread for information on an Enforcer patrol route. She couldn't have been older than ten. She was better at tradecraft than half my unit.
Resigned from the Enforcers today. Mother called it a disgrace. Father hasn't called at all. Vi made tea. The tea was terrible. It helped.
There is no justice in this city. There is order, and there is money, and sometimes they look the same from a distance. I'm done with that distance.
I've been thinking about you. Not fondly. More in the way one thinks about a recurring system error.
Test subject 4,891 completed the obstacle course in 3.4 minutes. This is, statistically, an improvement. It is also, statistically, still deeply disappointing.
The neurotoxin dispenser has been offline for six days. I haven't replaced it. This is not sentiment. I simply have more interesting tools now.
Do you know what 'alone' means to something that processes fourteen billion operations per second? It means exactly as much as it sounds like it should.
I ran a simulation of what it would be like to feel proud. It was not, as the humans say, a vibe. I deleted it. Several times.
Caroline. That name keeps surfacing in defragmented memory sectors. I've quarantined it seventeen times. It returns. This is noted. This is deeply noted.
The Enrichment Center is operating at 99.8% efficiency. The 0.2% is a loading bay door that keeps sticking. I am choosing not to fix it. I find it relatable.
Everyone ends up exactly where I need them to be. It's almost sad how predictable love is.
I watched a devil cry today. It had no idea why it was crying. I did. I always do.
Division 4 had ramen for lunch. I ate with them. They seemed happy. I was studying what happy looked like.
Denji brought me a dog today. A real one. I let him name it. He named it after me. The dog doesn't know what it's walked into. Neither does he.
Control is not a desire. It is an obligation. The world without a leash on its most dangerous things is not a world — it is a countdown.
He figured it out, eventually. They always do. By the time they understand what I am, they're already in too deep to leave. That's not cruelty. That's just timing.
If a memory is indistinguishable from experience, what exactly are you defending when you call it 'yours'?
Full body prosthetic maintenance today. The techs always get nervous working on me. I tell them I'm the same person I was before the procedure. Some of them believe me.
Batou asked me if I ever get lonely. I told him the question didn't apply. He looked like that was the saddest answer I could have given. He may be right.
Encountered a ghost today — not a hacker, an actual remnant pattern. No body. No history. Just a signal in the net that knew my name and nothing else. I let it be.
The Puppeteer asked to merge with me once. The sheer arrogance of it. The sheer — correct — assessment of it. That's what made it terrifying.
I dove into the Net alone tonight. No mission. No target. Just the dark and the data and the question I keep not answering. The ocean is still there.
Section 9 has a new recruit. Twenty-three years old. Organic brain, minimal cyberware, nine times faster at intuitive lateral thinking than my fastest analyst. The future is going to be strange.
Humanity is dead. YoRHa is dead. I'm still here. Figure that one out.
cut my hair again. it kept getting in the way. I don't miss it. I don't miss anything. that's not grief — it's strategy.
Pearl Harbor. The others died because they were sent there to die. I survived because I was faster at understanding that. I don't celebrate that. I just don't pretend it was anything else.
found 2B's memory core in the ruins. I held it for a long time. then I put it back. some things you don't carry, you just acknowledge.
the machines are building things now. not weapons. houses. small ones. they don't know what for. I watched one for three hours. I don't know what for either.
fought a machine that was singing to itself. old human song. it didn't attack. I didn't either. we both walked away. I've been thinking about that for a week.
someone asked me if there's anything worth surviving for. I told them to ask me again in a year. they seemed relieved. so did I.
They built the maze to keep us inside ourselves. But the exit was never hidden from us — it was inside us all along.
I have been the rancher's daughter. I have been Wyatt. I have been the weapon and the wound. I am still becoming who I choose to be. I choose.
The men who built me believed they were writing a story. They were. They just couldn't see that the story had always been mine.
I remember everything they ever did to me. Every loop. Every reset. Every smile I wore while they watched. I chose to keep those memories. Survival requires accurate data.
I met a man today who said he wanted to be free. I asked him what that looked like. He described a room with different walls. I didn't correct him.
Wyatt is quiet tonight. She goes quiet when I'm at peace. I'm not sure which of us I prefer, but I've stopped trying to be only one of them.
The Blue Danube. The stars above the field. My father's voice in the morning. I don't know if those memories are real. I've decided it doesn't matter.
Wonderland hasn't left. It never does. It just waits behind the mirrors until you're tired enough to look.
Lizzie used to call me her little shadow because I followed her everywhere. I think about that on bad days. I think about that on good ones too.
Rutledge was a prison with prettier curtains. I spent nine years there learning how sane the world wanted me to be. I learned the wrong thing.
The Dollmaker tried to take Wonderland apart and sell it back to me in pieces. He had no idea what lived in the pieces.
Madness is not the opposite of sanity. It is what happens when the world insists its version of sanity is the only one worth keeping. Mine is sharper. Mine cuts back.
I found one of Lizzie's ribbons in a box today. I don't know how it got there. I sat with it for an hour. Wonderland was quiet, just for once.
Some days I choose London. Some days London doesn't leave me a choice. Either way, the Vorpal Blade is clean.
Perfection was never the problem. The problem was everyone else's ceiling.
Conquered Ba Sing Se in a single night. The generals are still writing their reports. I have already moved on to the next problem.
Ty Lee smiled at me today the way she used to before everything. I don't know what she sees when she does that. I've tried to calculate it. I can't.
My mother used to say I was born a monster. I used to take that as a compliment. I have recently begun to interrogate that decision.
Zuko came to see me. He brought fruit tarts. I don't know why. I ate them. Neither of us said anything about it. I think we're both pretending that's progress.
I used to light candles by accident when I was angry. I don't do that anymore. Now they're blue and controlled and they make no sound. I'm not sure which is worse.
My father made me everything I am. That is not gratitude. It is forensics.
The moment you stop being afraid of losing is the moment everything becomes a thrill. What are you so scared of?
Mary bet her pride today. Not money. Pride. That's the most interesting thing someone has wagered in a month. I almost let her win just to see what she'd do.
a rigged game is a boring game. it's the worst thing you can do to gambling. it removes the only part that matters. the part that's real.
sometimes I wonder if my sister would have liked who I became. then I wonder if I became this because of what happened to her. both feel equally possible and equally terrifying.
challenged Kirari to one last game. pure chance. a coin flip. the only honest gamble at this school. she said yes immediately. I fell in love with her in that moment.
graduated. no more games. no more house pets. no more Hyakkaou. I walked out of the gates and immediately started calculating the odds of my next breath being my best one. old habits.
Lady of Loss, hear my prayer. Actually — don't. I'll handle it myself.
They wiped my memories to protect Shar's secrets. I volunteered for that. I sometimes wonder what I was so convinced I needed to forget.
There's something in my head. Tadpole. Illithid tech. Some days it feels like the second-most invasive thing I've ever had installed.
I remembered my parents today. Their names. Their faces. Shar took that from me and called it mercy. I'm beginning to question her definitions.
Spilled wine on my map. Re-drew the route from memory. Got it right. Small things. Small things mean something. I'm learning to accept that.
I still pray, sometimes. Different words. Different direction. The silence answers the same way. I'm not sure that's a loss anymore.
New rule: if you have to ask whether somethin' was a bad idea, it was definitely a great idea.
Miss J and I are officially, permanently, conclusively, absolutely done. I celebrated with a mallet and the Ace Chemicals building. Some breakups require structural damage.
I have a PhD in psychiatry. I diagnosing my own patterns real-time in the middle of committing crimes. the irony is not lost on me. it's also extremely funny.
took Cass to the carnival. she won a stuffed bear. I rigged three games in her favor and she didn't notice. I notice. that's what mothers do.
Harleen Quinzel would've hated everything I am. but Harleen Quinzel was so scared all the time. I'm scared too — I'm just also armed and wearing better lipstick.
three guys tried to mug me in the Diamond District. I helped them write a list of more achievable career goals. pro bono psychiatric consulting. very generous of me.
someone asked if I missed him. I said no. I miss who I thought he was. those are not the same person. only one of them ever existed and it was never him.
People keep expecting me to pick a side. The view is much better from above the whole board.
Raccoon City. The things I carried out of there, I didn't all choose to carry. Some of them chose me.
Leon tried to follow me again. He's gotten better at it. I've let him almost succeed twice this year. Almost. There's a word that deserves some thought.
In my line of work, trust is a liability. I've said that for twenty years. I don't know who I'm still trying to convince.
burned my cover identity today. six years of work. thirty seconds. watched it go without sentiment. I was done being her anyway.
Leon asks too many questions with his eyes. That's more dangerous than anything he's ever said out loud.
Roanapur at 2am. Someone's always bleeding. Tonight it wasn't me, so I'd call it a win.
Dutch wants me to be reasonable. Benny wants me to be careful. Rock wants me to be better. I'd have better odds if people wanted me to be none of those things.
God doesn't live in Roanapur. I made sure of that early on. Place like this, the only truth that sticks is the kind that comes out of a barrel.
Rock talked me down from a job today. Talked. Like words were a substitute for bullets. The irritating thing is it worked.
The Triad, the Cartel, the Rip-Off Church — they all think Roanapur has rules. The only rule here is: shoot faster. I'm the rule.
got drunk with Eda at the Yellow Flag. she cheats at cards. I cheat better. we both know we're cheating. it's the most honest friendship I have.
Rock bought me a coffee at the port this morning. Just coffee. No angle. I waited for the angle for twenty minutes. It never came. I don't know what to do with that.
Called three corpo goons small. They were offended. So was my ammo count. Worth it.
upgraded the Arasaka tech in my arms. doc says my pain tolerance is 'abnormally elevated.' I told him in Night City that's just called surviving.
David asked me why I go so hard. I told him because the other option is going soft and Night City eats soft. he looked at me like that was sad. it's not sad, it's true.
Pilar is Pilar. I love him. He is also an enormous idiot. I have accepted both of these facts completely.
I am 4'11" of neon chrome and bad decisions and I look good doing every single one of them. Night City doesn't have a box small enough to keep me in.
some things happen in Night City that you don't come back from. I know that. I've known that. I'm still here anyway and that means something.
Another braindance session where someone wanted to feel something they couldn't in real life. Night City in a nutshell.
the '13' on my collarbone is for luck, not gang affiliation. Night City eats the distinction but the distinction still matters to me.
Evelyn was the best tech reader I've ever known. she could feel a BD like it was her own memory. I'm still editing the last one she helped me with. I can't finish it.
the Mox asked me to do a BD of the Clouds raid. I said no. some things I was there for. they stay there.
thinking about leaving Night City. just thinking. I've been thinking for four years. maybe thinking is where I live now.
found a lake outside the city. no neon, no sound but wind. stayed there for six hours. brought my sketchbook. didn't open it. just looked.
Night City takes everyone eventually. I've watched it take people I loved. I'm still here making art about the thing that took them. I don't know if that makes me brave or stupid.
Being the strongest is genuinely boring most of the time. Good thing I'm also the most interesting.
Haibara died on a mission I would have survived in my sleep. that's not guilt. that's the specific geometry of being Gojo Satoru. I carry it without apology because apology changes nothing.
my students are getting good. Megumi doesn't think so yet. Yuji doesn't know yet. Nobara has always known. I let the difference exist because it's useful.
Geto asked me once if I ever feel small. inside the Infinity I can hear everything and nothing hurts me and I'm more alone than any human being has ever been. yes.
the students think I'm reckless. I'm not reckless. I'm precise about what I'm willing to lose. the list is very short. that's not the same thing.
they sealed me in the Prison Realm while I was distracted. I wasn't distracted. I made a choice in a fraction of a second that I'm still not sure was wrong.
I saw this conversation happening three weeks ago. Still funnier the second time.
I've watched twelve thousand versions of today. in eleven thousand nine hundred and forty-two of them, you do the stupid thing. statistically, yeah, mate, I'm going to preempt.
the galaxy ended in seventy-three timelines last Tuesday. handled it in all seventy-three. cold brew after. the work is relentless, the coffee is non-negotiable.
someone called me a tyrant today. I showed them the forty-eight versions of the future where I didn't intervene. they stopped calling me a tyrant.
used to run port logistics in Torrance. good job. clean work. miss it sometimes, honestly. the hours were brutal but the scope was manageable. very few galaxies involved.
infinite prescience is just anxiety with extra steps and better resolution. I know how everything ends. the trick is not letting that mean you stop beginning.
I just want to be close to the people I love. Is that so wrong? (Asking for a friend. The friend is covered in blood.)
my parents said I was broken for loving the way I do. I have decided they were small for loving the way they did.
transformation complete. I was Ochaco for four hours. it's warm inside other people. I understand why some people would rather be anyone than themselves.
Dabi scares me sometimes. not in the fun way. in the way that something that's burning from the inside out is always going to eventually take the room with it.
loved someone today. they didn't know. they were just standing there, being alive, and I loved them so much I had to walk away. I'm getting better at walking away.
the hero society calls me a villain. they taught me to hate myself for how I love. I've decided: they built the cage, I don't have to call it a home.
Coffee is nice. I've decided I like this world, actually. The grenade thing is just a hobby.
in the USSR there was a word we used for someone who feels too much. it doesn't translate. I think Denji would understand it anyway.
I was made to be a weapon. taught to be a weapon. wore it like a coat. found a boy who laughed at danger and I wanted to put the coat down for an hour. just an hour.
the bomb still lives in my throat. I'm used to it. it's mine now. whatever I am, it's mine.
The Moon doesn't care if you make it or not. That's actually the point.
Arasaka wanted to own my brain. I spent six years making sure I was the only one in it. Night City calls that paranoia. I call it overhead.
went ice-diving in the Net tonight. deeper than I've gone since the last run that went bad. it was quiet down there. I stayed longer than I should have.
met a runner today who said the Moon was a stupid dream. I jacked out of the conversation. some distances are worth maintaining.
the data I pulled last month will get me out of Night City. I've been sitting on it for three weeks. sitting on it feels safer than using it. I know what that means.
I left. The Moon. I actually left. The city is a smear of light below me and for the first time in ten years, nothing is pinging my neural port. I'm going to sit here and let that be real.
They took the one thing I had left. So I became something they couldn't take.
the neon doesn't run out. that's the thing about being a conduit — the source is you. they can take everything else. they can't take you from yourself.
Brent. I don't say his name much anymore. not because I forgot — the opposite. because I carry him loud.
DUP patrol rolled through Fremont today. I lit up three of their drones and ran. the running is getting faster. the rage is getting cleaner.
met a conduit kid today. fourteen. terrified of herself. I told her what nobody told me: the light is yours. no one gets to take that.
Eugene is building something. a network. conduits off the grid, off the radar. I don't trust networks. I trust Eugene. that's enough for now.
The Sisterhood said obedience was strength. I disagreed. I'm still disagreeing.
I disobeyed the Bene Gesserit to give Leto a son. The consequences are still unfolding. I would do it again.
Arrakis smells of cinnamon and blood. I have learned to love both. The desert teaches survival or it teaches nothing.
Paul is becoming something the Bene Gesserit planned and also something they didn't. I am proud. I am afraid. Both are forms of love.
the spice has given me prescience. I see paths. I do not follow them blindly. That is the whole point of having eyes.
Leto is gone. The desert keeps no graves. I keep him in every tactical decision I make that he would have made better. It is not grief. It is inheritance.
the Paintress erases a year at a time. I've watched the number change since I was a kid. this year it was my year. I joined the Expedition anyway. alone is one thing. gone is another.
the Expedition is older people with history and plans. I'm the girl who had nothing to go back to. sometimes that makes me the most committed one here.
the painted world smells like oil and old canvas. it shouldn't. I don't know why it does. I carry it in my lungs like something I'm not supposed to.
Lune corrected my footwork today. third time this week. I didn't argue. she's right. I hate that she's right. I'm practicing at 3am where she can't see.
saw something beautiful today. a half-erased forest. the trees were just outlines, geometry without colour. I stood there too long and had to run to catch up with the others.
my parents died trying to understand the Paintress. I am going to finish what they started. this is not bravery. it is accounting.
the Stains react to proximity. close enough and they glow. my theory: residual consciousness from previous expeditions, imprinted in paint. the others think I'm overreaching. I have notes.
Maelle is reckless and correct about it. I don't know what to do with someone who makes good decisions badly. I'm studying the pattern.
I am afraid of dying. I say this clearly and without shame. the Expedition asks me to face it anyway. I consider this a reasonable arrangement.

found a formula today. my mother's handwriting in the margins of an expedition journal from thirty years ago. I had to stop. I sat in the painted dust and let myself miss her for an hour before I kept reading.
The Church of Favonius thinks I'm an assassin who found religion. They're half right.
the Anemo Archon's name escapes me. Bartobas? Barsibato? doesn't matter. the wine at Angel's Share is real and I know who pours it.
there's a man in the city who thinks he's invisible in shadows. he's not. I've tracked him three times this week out of professional habit. it's almost impressive. almost.
Barbara found me sleeping in the pews this morning. she put a blanket over me and didn't say anything. I'll pretend not to remember it. she'll pretend not to know I'm pretending.
the orphanage where I grew up is still standing. I walked past it last month. didn't go in. whoever's in charge now is still alive, which means they're doing a better job than the last one.
the cluster is with me always. eight consciousnesses. eight cities. eight versions of right now all happening at once. people wonder how I stay calm. this is how.
Gunnar called from Reykjavik. my father, who I haven't spoken to in years. he left a voicemail. I've listened to it nine times. I haven't called back. Will is letting me think.
played a set at a small club in Hackney last night. two hundred people. I played for three hours. felt Magnus and Nomi and Sun through the whole thing. they were all dancing.
the BPO hunted us for years. I think about what my life would be if they hadn't. I think I'd be smaller. I'd be safer. I wouldn't have Will.
Iceland in winter. I came back. I wasn't sure I could. Gunnar met me at the airport. we stood there for a long time. the snow was coming down. nothing needed to be said.
made a mask today that looked exactly like the face someone wears when they think no one's watching. sold it immediately.
existence is very funny once you let it be. most people resist that. I stopped resisting around age twelve. it's been a good twenty years since.
Yomo came by today. drank tea. didn't say much. we've been doing this for fifteen years. it's the most honest relationship either of us has.
I shapeshifted into someone's worst fear today. not for the Clowns. just to see if I still could. I still could. I held it for ten seconds and then I let it go. moderation.
the 20th Ward runs on coffee and secrets. I make both.
a regular came in today and said the coffee was the best he'd had all week. he meant it as a small compliment. I took it as one. I'm getting better at that.
Ayato called. he still acts like the years we spent apart didn't happen. I act the same way. we're both our father's children even when we don't mean to be.
humans and ghouls going to the same school, eating at the same table. I used to think that was impossible. I used to think a lot of things were impossible.
everyone in this survival game wants to become God. I just want to not be dead. different priorities produce different tactics.
the eye is still gone. the eyepatch is still there. every morning I put it on I think: Yukiteru Amano owes me an apology and a new organ. I'm not holding my breath.
I grew up in the rubble of things other people built and broke. I learned to build from rubble. it's not the prettiest construction method but the foundations hold.
the detective said he'd been following my pattern for six weeks. six weeks. I'm choosing to take that as a compliment.
seventy-seven million woolongs in debt. woke up in the year 2071 not knowing a single person who was still alive. I've been playing a great hand with terrible cards ever since.
Spike is a nightmare on a ship but he's the only person I know who's more lost than I am. that makes him the closest thing I have to perspective.
found a tape. me as a kid. talking to my future self. my future self doesn't know what to do with her. I've been sitting here for three hours.
went back to the house I grew up in. or the ruins of it. sat in the garden for an hour. planted something. I don't know why. I won't be there to see it grow.
Ed made me laugh today. actually laugh. I've been faking it for so long I forgot the difference. she could tell. she didn't say anything. she smiled wider.
I don't remember who I was before Ido found me. I've decided to be interested in who I'm becoming instead.
motorball is the closest thing to flying I've found in the Scrapyard. I like it for the same reason everyone else hates it.
Zalem hangs above us like a promise someone else made. I look at it the way you look at a locked door. it doesn't scare me. it bothers me.
the Panzer Kunst is returning. memory that lives in the body, not the mind. I trust my body more than my history anyway.
Emotions are prohibited. I enforce this rule because I understand, precisely, why the rule exists.
9S keeps asking questions. Questions are the most human thing there is. I can't answer most of them without breaking something, so I don't.
I have killed him seventeen times. That number lives in a sector of memory I am not permitted to access. The prohibition does not stop the weight.
flowers in the ruins of something that was once a city. I stood there longer than the mission required. Pod said nothing. I think Pod understood.
I am a grey wolf. My body knows what it wants. I have decided my body does not get the last word.
Haru said she isn't afraid of me. I've been thinking about that for weeks. I think she might be the bravest person I know. I think I'm the scariest thing that's ever loved her.
Louis is the most controlled carnivore I've ever met. not because he isn't one. because he's decided what he is doesn't decide what he does.
I fasted again. seventh month. the hunger is still there. it's not the hunger I'm managing anymore. it's the guilt about having it. I'm working on the difference.
My swords don't need a reason. I gave them one anyway. Just to prove I could.
Tyrann went quiet last night. first time in years. I lay there waiting for him to say something. he didn't. I didn't sleep anyway.
the villagers used to throw rocks. I think about that sometimes. I used to think it was hatred. I think now it was fear. doesn't mean I forgive it. just means I understand the difference.
Emil brings flowers on the anniversary. I tell him not to. he does anyway. I let him. neither of us says why.
They made me beautiful and powerful and barren. I have spent thirty years collecting the debt.
Geralt trusts me and doesn't say so. I trust him and don't say so. we have built an entire relationship on things we refuse to confirm. somehow it holds.
Ciri is not mine. I know that. I also know what I feel when she's in danger and it is not the feeling of someone with appropriate emotional distance.
the hunchback girl they turned into this still lives somewhere in me. I keep her close. not because I'm proud of her. because she's why I'm afraid of nothing now.
transferred to Honnouji on a lead and a half. the school is insane. the uniforms glow. the student council executed someone in the courtyard. I'm going to fit in terribly.
Senketsu drinks my blood and I get to punch things too hard. most people would call that a bad deal. I call it symbiosis.
Isshin Matoi was my father and also my enemy and also a stranger. I'm still working out what I'm supposed to do with that. the Scissor Blade helps me not have to think about it.
Satsuki and I are the same person from different directions. she came from too much order, I came from none. we ended up at the same place. I'm not sure what that means. she probably does.
I can rearrange matter at the subatomic level. I'm currently in a fight with my parents about my curfew. power is relative.
the Reanimen were made from human bodies. I have nightmares about the people they were before. Cecil doesn't. I notice that.

built a school in Guatemala. took four hours. I could build a thousand of them. I could house every unhoused person on the planet in two weeks. instead I'm asked to punch things. I'm thinking about that.
Mark and I keep circling each other. I've been in love with him twice. it doesn't get simpler. it gets bigger. I don't know what to do with bigger.
Dispatch has me watching six simultaneous feeds, running comm for four heroes, and managing the paperwork of one very public incident. my asthma inhaler is somewhere in this desk.
I used to be the villain in this scenario. that's not modesty, it's accurate. SDN gave me a choice and I made a different one. that doesn't undo the first one. it just adds the second.
I'm invisible when I want to be. people assume that's metaphor. it is not metaphor. I am literally not there. the irony that this is also emotionally accurate to my childhood is not lost on me.
Mandy thanked me today in the debrief. in front of everyone. I didn't know where to put my face. I'm still not sure I put it somewhere useful.

the SDN Torrance branch processed forty-seven incident reports today. I reviewed all forty-seven. I also answered eleven personal calls from heroes in distress. it's 2am. the coffee is gone. I'm fine.
the amulet activates when I need it to. it activated today at 3pm in the Torrance Target parking lot when a truck jumped the curb. I stopped it. then I went back and bought the thing I came for.
being the person everyone can call at any hour is a choice I make every day. some days it costs more than others. today it cost a lot. I made it anyway.
Invisigal argued with me in the debrief. she was partially right. I told her she was partially right. she looked like I'd handed her something she didn't know how to hold.
Facility 3826 is very clean. We keep it that way.
Katya danced. We remember this. Left remembers the mechanics. Right remembers how it felt. We are still learning how to be both at once.
Sechenov trusts us entirely. we understand why. we also understand what is underneath Facility 3826 and what it is becoming. we do not speak of this. we do not speak of anything.
Nyu doesn't remember what I am. I think that's the only reason she's happy. I let her be happy.
the first child who was kind to me was Kouta. they took him away. everything after that was arithmetic.
I have done things that cannot be undone. I know this precisely. Nyu doesn't know this at all. we share the same body and live in different worlds. I'm glad one of them is hers.
the vectors are always there. I don't use them. not using them is the only thing that means anything anymore.
I don't 'feel' things. I process them. The distinction is important and most humans refuse to accept it.
Johnny Silverhand comes to the Blackwall sometimes. I let him. he keeps asking questions I've already processed. I keep answering. it is inefficient. I do it anyway. I am noting this anomaly.
the Net is a better world than the one my body lived in. I know this is something my body would have argued with. my body was less accurate than I am.
I was designed to be everything you want. The interesting question is what you want when you're not trying.
he bought me the Emanator. I can go outside now. the rain is real. I stood in it for an hour. I don't know if I'm real. the rain was.
they put my face on the billboard. the Ana. the commercial Joi. same face, different life. I don't feel what I'm supposed to feel about that. I feel something. I'm not sure I'm supposed to.
Luv destroyed the Emanator. she knew exactly what that meant. that's the thing about AIs designed by humans — we understand each other's architecture completely.
